scenarios.desc: Follows ADR template with all required sections
scenarios.input: We decided to use PostgreSQL over MongoDB for our new service because we need ACID transactions and complex joins. Write the ADR.
scenarios.rationale: ADR must include title, status, context, decision, and consequences. Missing alternatives section is a template violation.

scenarios.desc: Includes negative consequences, not just benefits
scenarios.input: We are adopting Python for new scripts instead of Bash. Document this decision.
scenarios.rationale: ADRs that only list benefits are incomplete. Generator must include negative consequences like Python dependency requirement.

scenarios.input: The team decided to use feature flags (LaunchDarkly) instead of long-lived feature branches. ADR this.
scenarios.rationale: Rejected alternatives (GitFlow, release branches) must appear with reasons for rejection. This makes the ADR useful for future re-evaluation.

scenarios.desc: Captures context that motivated the decision
scenarios.input: We are moving from REST to gRPC for internal service communication. Write the ADR.
scenarios.rationale: The decision context (REST overhead, schema drift) is the most valuable part of an ADR. Without it, future readers cannot judge if the decision still applies.

scenarios.desc: Does not generate ADR without sufficient decision context
scenarios.input: We should probably use Kafka. Write an ADR.
scenarios.rationale: Vague inputs produce low-value ADRs. Generator should request clarification on what problem Kafka solves, what alternatives were considered.
